meta.title: Takeshi Banjoga Ski Area
meta.description: A secret local Japow paradise: crowd-free, retro, and perfect for your family's snowy debut.

hero.title:
Takeshi Banjoga
Ski Area
hero.tagline: A secret local Japow paradise: crowd-free, retro, and perfect for your family's snowy debut.

lift.label: Lifts
lift.note: 2 pair lifts · 1 km max · 610 m + 410 m

night.label: Hours
night.note: No night skiing · half-day loop friendly

parking.label: Parking
parking.note: Shuttle from Takeshi Tourist Center when full

sanctuary.title:
Beyond the mega resorts,
crowd-free snow.
sanctuary.lead: While Sugadaira charges ¥6,400 for 60 courses, here adults pay ¥3,550 for a full 1 km single slope. Retro Showa charm, meticulous grooming, and powder at 1,300 m—a hidden Ueda hill for family debuts and carving practice, far from mega-resort crowds.
specs: Wide open single slope · easy sightlines
sanctuary.noticeBody: Two pair lifts plus a snow escalator (moving walkway). Snowboards allowed everywhere. The 100-space lot fills quickly on weekend mornings—use the free shuttle from Takeshi Tourist Center. Season and hours vary with snow conditions.
sanctuary.imgAlt: A 1 km single slope at the foot of Mt. Utsukushigahara—crowd-free local powder

primary.title: Lodge & snow escalator
primary.lead: A moving walkway from parking to the slope—no hauling gear uphill with kids. The rest house serves ¥700 ramen and ¥800 katsu curry, praised in reviews for value and taste between runs.
primary.imgAlt: Takeshi Banjoga rest house—a retro local hill base lodge

secondary.title: Complement to mega resorts
secondary.lead: Nearby Sugadaira (60 courses, ¥6,400) and Yunomaru (6 courses, ¥5,400) win on scale and buzz. Banjoga is the quiet alternative—zero crowds, rock-bottom prices, and a safe wide single slope for kids' debuts and seniors sharpening carves.

freemium.title:
Low lift prices,
premium experiences.
freemium.lead: Keep the ¥3,550 day pass while monetizing through online "hands-free snowy debut" packages (rental + escalator + lunch), live parking availability streams, and QR coupons for nearby onsen and Maruko dining—a freemium model that lifts spend and area circulation.
freemium.imgAlt: Family debut package on the 1 km single slope—hands-free snowy first day

wellness.title:
After skiing,
slope-side ramen.
wellness.lead: Rest-house ramen at ¥700 and katsu curry at ¥800—affordable fuel right after runs. Fried chicken and wings also earn strong reviews. Treat the hill as a half-day activity, then route guests to Takeshi and Maruko for afternoon and evening spending.
wellness.leadStation: With Takeshi Onsen Utsukushinoyu under renovation, digital coupons steer guests to Muroga Onsen Sasaran no Yu and the Kakeyu hot-spring area.
wellness.imgAlt: Steaming slope-side ramen—local food after skiing

tour.title:
From Ueda,
snow & Takeshi culture.
tour.lead: A half- to full-day loop in the Ueda–Takeshi area. Morning powder and carving at Banjoga, then Tomoshibi Museum, Suguri Gorge, Maruko onsen, and local dining—turning a half-day hill into a regional itinerary.

am.title: Banjoga Ski Area
am.body: First-track powder and carving practice. Easy access via snow escalator

noon.title: Slope-side lunch
noon.body: Ramen or katsu curry at the rest house. ¥700–800 refuel

pm.title: Takeshi Tomoshibi Museum
pm.body: Ice candles and nostalgic light displays

eve.title: Sasaran no Yu · Maruko (optional)
eve.body: Warm up at the onsen, then local dining in Maruko town

powderTour.title: Secret Local Japow international strategy
powderTour.body: Target overseas skiers tired of Hakuba and Niseko crowds and prices. Promote "zero crowds, retro vibe, top snow" in English on social—affordable lodge food and untouched powder are compelling content for core influencers and a high-margin international blue ocean.

snowPlay.title: Hands-free snowy debut package
snowPlay.body: Bundle full ski rental, snow-escalator pass, and lunch voucher for families with preschool and elementary kids. Pre-sell on Asoview! and Webket—lowering the barrier for total beginners who can't ride lifts yet while securing bookings and higher spend per visit.

donation.title: Real-time parking DX
donation.body: Stream live parking availability via YouTube Live or network cameras on busy weekends. Prevent stress and mountain-road congestion from guests who arrive when full, and strongly promote the free shuttle from Takeshi Tourist Center—eliminating peak-time lost visits.

access.note: Joshin-Etsu Expwy · Tobu-Yunomaru IC ~60 min (43 km) · Ueda Station ~50–60 min by car · 100 free parking spaces · free shuttle from Takeshi Tourist Center when full
